ipc_sections.title: Attempt to Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

ipc_sections.title: Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: An assembly of five or more persons is designated an 'unlawful assembly' if the common object is to overawe Government, resist execution of legal process, commit mischief, criminal trespass, or other offences.

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Attempt to Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

pendingCasesDetails.court: Principle District Court, Salem, PDJ Court Krtishnagiri, then Transferred to Salem on 24.01.2024

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Every Member of Unlawful Assembly Guilty of Offence Committed in Prosecution of Common Object
ipc_sections.description: If an offence is committed by any member of an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of that assembly, every person who is a member of the same assembly is guilty of that offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence committed

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Attempt to Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Theft

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Attempt to Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

ipc_sections.title: Every Member of Unlawful Assembly Guilty of Offence Committed in Prosecution of Common Object
ipc_sections.description: If an offence is committed by any member of an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of that assembly, every person who is a member of the same assembly is guilty of that offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence committed

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_sections.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Forgery for Purpose of Cheating
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Using as Genuine a Forged Document or Electronic Record
ipc_sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document or electronic record which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document or electronic record.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for forgery of such document

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking in Order to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass or house-breaking, in order to the committing of any offence punishable with imprisonment.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine; 10 years if theft

ipc_sections.title: Theft in Dwelling House
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel, which building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling, or used for the custody of property.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust by Public Servant, or by Banker, Merchant or Agent
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property in his capacity of a public servant or in the way of his business as a banker, merchant, factor, broker, attorney or agent, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of that property.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Mischief Causing Damage to the Amount of Fifty Rupees
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits mischief and thereby causes loss or damage to the amount of fifty rupees or upwards.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Cr.No. 73/2019, Kenikarai Police Station, Ramanathapuram District

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Fire or Combustible Matter
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does, with fire or any combustible matter, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Intentional Insult with Intent to Provoke Breach of the Peace
ipc_sections.description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Bribery
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of bribery shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both; provided that bribery by treating shall be punished with fine only.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No. 527/2024 Thirupathur City Police Station

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Escape from Confinement or Custody Negligently Suffered by Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, legally bound to keep in confinement any person charged with or convicted of any offence or lawfully committed to custody, negligently suffers such person to escape from confinement.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Sentence of Forfeiture of Property
ipc_sections.description: Historical provision.

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: When Such an Act Is Criminal by Reason of Being Done with Criminal Knowledge or Intention
ipc_sections.description: Whenever an act, which is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention, is done by several persons, each of such persons who joins in the act with such knowledge or intention is liable for the act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone with that knowledge or intention.

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Mischief Causing Damage to the Amount of Fifty Rupees
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits mischief and thereby causes loss or damage to the amount of fifty rupees or upwards.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

ipc_sections.title: Year and Month
ipc_sections.description: Wherever the word 'year' or 'month' is used, it is to be understood that the year or the month is to be reckoned according to the British calendar.

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Abetment of Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life — If Offence Not Committed
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 14 years and fine if hurt caused

sub_sections.description: If any act for which the abettor is liable in consequence of the abetment, and which causes hurt to any person, is done, the abettor shall be liable to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine.

ipc_sections.title: Concealing Design to Commit Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby facilitate the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, voluntarily conceals the existence of a design to commit such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 10 years if committed by public servant

ipc_sections.title: Assault
ipc_sections.description: Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit an assault.

ipc_sections.title: Forgery of Valuable Security, Will
ipc_sections.description: Whoever forges a document purporting to be a valuable security or a will, or an authority to adopt a son, or which purports to give authority to any person to make or transfer any valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Forgery for Purpose of Cheating
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Using as Genuine a Forged Document or Electronic Record
ipc_sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document or electronic record which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document or electronic record.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for forgery of such document

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_sections.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No. 274/2016 Inspector of Police, Bolur

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Disobedience to Order Duly Promulgated by Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, knowing that, by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, he is directed to abstain from a certain act or to take certain order with certain property in his possession or under his management, disobeys such direction.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if danger to human life

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Continuance of Nuisance After Injunction to Discontinue
ipc_sections.description: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance, having been enjoined by any public servant who has lawful authority to issue such injunction not to repeat or continue such nuisance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Sale of Obscene Books
ipc_sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or in any manner puts into circulation, or for purposes of sale, hire, distribution, public exhibition or circulation, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 5 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly

ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Public Nuisance in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Code.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Escape from Confinement or Custody Negligently Suffered by Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, legally bound to keep in confinement any person charged with or convicted of any offence or lawfully committed to custody, negligently suffers such person to escape from confinement.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Commencement and Continuance of the Right of Private Defence of Property
ipc_sections.description: The right of private defence of property commences when a reasonable apprehension of danger to the property commences and it continues while such reasonable apprehension of danger to the property continues.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Person Abetted Does Act with Different Intention from That of Abettor
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence shall, if the person abetted does the act with a different intention from that of the abettor, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence which would have been committed if the act had been done with the intention of the abettor.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted with abettor's intention

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, legally bound to keep in confinement any person charged with or convicted of any offence or lawfully committed to custody, negligently suffers such person to escape from confinement.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Fire or Combustible Matter
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does, with fire or any combustible matter, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Abetment of Mutiny if Mutiny Is Committed in Consequence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the committing of mutiny and mutiny is committed in consequence of that abetment.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

ipc_sections.title: Personating a Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever pretends to hold any particular office as a public servant, knowing that he does not hold such office, and in such assumed character does or attempts to do any act under colour of such office.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Delivery of Coin Possessed with Knowledge That It Is Counterfeit
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, having knowledge that any coin is counterfeit, delivers such coin to any person, or attempts to induce any person to receive it.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No-183 of 2024, J-4 Kotturpuram Police Station

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No- 190 of 2022, E-2, Royapettah Police Station

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Act Endangering Life or Personal Safety of Others
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 250 or both

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No- 387of 2021 , D-4, Zam Bazaar Police Station

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No- 347 of 2023 J-7 Guindy Police Station

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Knowingly Joining or Continuing in Assembly of Five or More Persons After It Has Been Commanded to Disperse
ipc_sections.description: Whoever knowingly joins or continues in any assembly of five or more persons likely to cause a disturbance of the public peace, after such assembly has been lawfully commanded to disperse.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No- 302 of 2022 , F-3 Nungambakkam Police Station

ipc_sections.title: Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being aware of facts which render any assembly an unlawful assembly, intentionally joins that assembly, or continues in it, is said to be a member of an unlawful assembly.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Public Nuisance in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Code.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

ipc_sections.title: Continuance of Nuisance After Injunction to Discontinue
ipc_sections.description: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance, having been enjoined by any public servant who has lawful authority to issue such injunction not to repeat or continue such nuisance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

pendingCasesDetails.other_details: Section-41 (6) Madras City Police Station Act

ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_sections.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_sections.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Forgery of Valuable Security, Will
ipc_sections.description: Whoever forges a document purporting to be a valuable security or a will, or an authority to adopt a son, or which purports to give authority to any person to make or transfer any valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Forgery for Purpose of Cheating
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Using as Genuine a Forged Document or Electronic Record
ipc_sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document or electronic record which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document or electronic record.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for forgery of such document

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crime No 347/2008, Austinpatti Police Station Madurai District

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: Crome No 309/2003, Avaniapuram Police Station, Madurai City

ipc_sections.title: Making Preparation to Commit Dacoity
ipc_sections.description: Whoever makes any preparation for committing dacoity shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Assembling for Purpose of Committing Dacoity
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, at any time after the passing of this Act, shall be one of five or more persons assembled for the purpose of committing dacoity.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rash Driving or Riding on a Public Way
ipc_sections.description: Whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 335, voluntarily causes grievous hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Attempt to Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Confinement
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Every Member of Unlawful Assembly Guilty of Offence Committed in Prosecution of Common Object
ipc_sections.description: If an offence is committed by any member of an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of that assembly, every person who is a member of the same assembly is guilty of that offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence committed

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Sale of Obscene Books
ipc_sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or in any manner puts into circulation, or for purposes of sale, hire, distribution, public exhibition or circulation, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 5 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Breach of Trust
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
ipc_sections.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Sale of Obscene Books
ipc_sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or in any manner puts into circulation, or for purposes of sale, hire, distribution, public exhibition or circulation, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 5 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Machinery
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does, with any machinery, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Sale of Obscene Books
ipc_sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or in any manner puts into circulation, or for purposes of sale, hire, distribution, public exhibition or circulation, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 5 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Disobedience to Order Duly Promulgated by Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, knowing that, by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, he is directed to abstain from a certain act or to take certain order with certain property in his possession or under his management, disobeys such direction.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if danger to human life

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Dishonestly Breaking Open Receptacle Containing Property
ipc_sections.description: Whoever dishonestly, or with intent to commit mischief, breaks open or unfastens any closed receptacle which contains or which he believes to contain property.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Malignant Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever malignantly does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Public Nuisance in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Code.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

ipc_sections.title: Continuance of Nuisance After Injunction to Discontinue
ipc_sections.description: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance, having been enjoined by any public servant who has lawful authority to issue such injunction not to repeat or continue such nuisance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Illegal Payments in Connection with an Election
ipc_sections.description: Whoever without the authority of a candidate incurs or authorises expenses on account of the holding of any public meeting, or upon any advertisement, circular or publication for the purpose of promoting or procuring the election of such candidate.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 500

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Disobedience to Order Duly Promulgated by Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, knowing that, by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, he is directed to abstain from a certain act or to take certain order with certain property in his possession or under his management, disobeys such direction.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if danger to human life

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Malignant Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
ipc_sections.description: Whoever malignantly does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

pendingCasesDetails.other_details: Section- 51(a), 51(b) - DM Act 2005, Sec. 3 - ED ACT 1897

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: 89/2025, Arakkonam Taluk Police Station, Ranipet District.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Abetment of Mutiny if Mutiny Is Committed in Consequence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the committing of mutiny and mutiny is committed in consequence of that abetment.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

pendingCasesDetails.fir_no: 46/2026 Pallipattu D5 Police Station, Thiruvallur District.
pendingCasesDetails.court: District Munsif cum Judicial Magistrate Court - Pallipattu

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Wearing Garb or Carrying Token Used by Public Servant with Fraudulent Intent
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, not belonging to a class of public servants, wears any garb or carries any token resembling garb or token used by that class, with the intention that it may be believed that he belongs to that class.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 200 or both

ipc_sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Fire or Combustible Matter
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does, with fire or any combustible matter, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Sale of Obscene Books
ipc_sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or in any manner puts into circulation, or for purposes of sale, hire, distribution, public exhibition or circulation, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 5 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction
